item.title: Welcome our new robotaxi overlords
item.description: The arrival of driverless vehicles is a second chance for cities

item.title: The energy crisis is no excuse for bad subsidies
item.description: We know what worked and what failed the last time around

item.title: Puma replaces finance chief as group steps up turnaround plan
item.description: Chief executive Arthur Hoeld has cut hundreds of jobs and reshaped sportswear company’s leadership team

item.title: Magnum’s mini ice creams ease fears of weight-loss drug threat
item.description: Ben & Jerry’s maker beats first-quarter sales forecasts though its price growth was lower than expected

item.title: Air France owner increases prices as fuel costs surge by $2.4bn
item.description: Airline pares growth plans, cuts spending and increases ticket prices in response to Iran war

item.title: DCC rejects £5bn takeover bid from KKR and Energy Capital
item.description: FTSE 100 energy group says bid ‘fundamentally undervalues’ its business

item.title: Should you be worried about private credit?
item.description: John Foley and Sujeet Indap of the FT’s Lex column will answer your questions live on Thursday April 30 at 1pm BST

item.title: Apple’s new chief and China’s chilling move on Manus
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: Lazard to buy private capital advisory group in $575mn deal
item.description: Acquisition comes as private capital groups push into increasingly complicated transactions requiring advisory

item.title: VW takes €500mn hit from shelving EV production at US plant
item.description: German carmaker has shifted production at Tennessee plant to a petrol SUV

item.title: Whitbread to sell £1.5bn of Premier Inn properties after activist pressure
item.description: Group announces overhaul of restaurants business, putting 3,800 jobs at risk

item.title: The Fed’s balancing act
item.description: Plus tech earnings

item.title: French lenders miss out on trading boom powering Wall Street banks
item.description: BNP Paribas and Crédit Agricole report higher first-quarter profit but are unable to fully capitalise on market volatility

item.title: StanChart books $190mn charge on Iran war
item.description: Asia-focused bank reports record quarterly profit despite setting aside funds to hedge against Middle East risk

item.title: FirstFT: Google pulls ahead as Big Tech AI spending targets surge
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: oil surpasses $125 and Russia’s Mali setback

item.title: Private equity’s tempest in a teapot
item.description: CVC and KKR are facing the prospect of extended ownership of food businesses they acquired from Unilever

item.title: Bank of England in stand-off with FCA over trading firms’ capital
item.description: Rare dispute emerges after watchdog proposes to boost liquidity for groups such as Jane Street and Citadel Securities

item.title: UK’s Wycombe Abbey school forced to close China campus
item.description: Shutdown marks setback for international education groups looking to expand in China

item.title: UAExit adds momentum to the Opec doom loop
item.description: The Emirates’ departure reflects the cartel’s declining market share and the ongoing energy transition

item.title: KKR explores $10bn sale of ex-Unilever spreads business
item.description: Private capital firm hires bankers to sell Flora Food Group after ditching targets to make its portfolio plant based

item.title: Luxury yachtmaker’s CEO hits out at biggest shareholder’s ‘lack of vision’
item.description: Ferretti boss Alberto Galassi criticises Chinese investor Weichai as proxy battle intensifies at maker of Riva boats

item.title: AI companies are just companies
item.description: As we leap into a new technological age, the old rules of capitalism still apply

item.title: Jet fuel crisis lays bare faultlines in US airline industry
item.description: Delta and United cement dominance as low-cost carriers rue post-pandemic splurge on aircraft

item.title: Half of ‘long shot’ Polymarket bets on military action are successful
item.description: High rate of winning wagers likely to add to concerns that sensitive information can leak on prediction markets

item.title: How Revolut became a verb — if not yet a lender
item.description: Boss Nik Storonsky is hoping that a US banking licence and an IPO will sustain fintech’s breakneck growth

item.title: Prophecy — why predictions are all about power
item.description: Oxford philosopher Carissa Véliz’s ingenious, scathing survey of forecasting takes a well-timed swipe at today’s obsession with predictive algorithms

item.title: Defence stocks drop despite US-Iran war
item.description: Weapons makers’ shares slide as investors fear production bottlenecks and uncertainty over US defence funding

item.title: Shipbuilding feels pinch from war-driven paint and lubricant shortage
item.description: Supply bottlenecks mark the widening fallout from Iran conflict and its disruption on Middle East oil supplies

item.title: Tech results as it happened: Google, Meta and Microsoft boost AI spending forecasts
item.description: Facebook owner’s stock drops 6.5% while Alphabet, Microsoft and Amazon report strong cloud computing growth

item.title: Google outpaces rivals as Big Tech’s AI spending plans rise to $725bn
item.description: Meta stock drops on capex increase while Alphabet’s cloud business grows faster than rivals Amazon and Microsoft
